title: Verb ずにはすまない
structure: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはすまない
structureEn: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはすまない
explanation: 'Cannot do without...', 'unavoidable', 'have to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point ずにはすまない is used when something must be done due to social/moral obligation, necessity, or rules. It implies that there is no way around taking that action.

title: Verb そうにない
titleEn: Expresses unlikelihood or that something does not seem likely to happen.
explanation: Expresses unlikelihood or that something does not seem likely to happen. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The そうにない grammar point is typically formed from the verb’s ます-stem plus そうにない, and is used to express that something seems unlikely to happen or that someone seems unlikely to do something.

title: Verb そうもない
titleEn: Expresses negative potential; 'it doesn't seem like...', 'it's not likely...'.
explanation: Expresses negative potential; 'it doesn't seem like...', 'it's not likely...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: ～そうもない is used to express a low possibility or unlikelihood of something happening. It is typically attached to the ます-stem of a verb, similar to そうにない but with an even stronger sense of improbability in many contexts.

title: Verb そばから
titleEn: Expresses that as soon as one action finishes, another begins immediately.
explanation: Expresses that as soon as one action finishes, another begins immediately. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The 〜そばから grammar point is used to indicate that right after a certain action is completed, another action takes place—often suggesting a repetitive or frustrating cycle.

title: Verb たが最後
titleEn: Emphasizes 'once something happens, there is no going back'; 'once', 'as soon as', etc.
structure: Verb-casual, past (た form) + が最後
structureEn: Verb-casual, past (た form) + が最後
explanation: Emphasizes 'once something happens, there is no going back'; 'once', 'as soon as', etc. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The 〜たが最後 grammar point indicates that once an action is taken or event occurs, some (often irreversible) consequence follows. In English, it can be translated as 'once', 'as soon as', or 'the moment that...'.

title: Verb たことにしてください
titleEn: Asking someone to pretend or accept that something has already happened or taken a certain state.
structure: Verb (past, casual) + ことにしてください
structureEn: Verb (past, casual) + ことにしてください
explanation: Asking someone to pretend or accept that something has already happened or taken a certain state. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point 'Verb たことにしてください' is used to request someone to act as if or pretend that a certain action has already occurred or a certain state exists. It is used in various contexts to express the idea of accepting a hypothetical situation for convenience or to avoid conflicts.

title: Verb たら Verb たで
titleEn: Conveys that if something happens (or doesn’t happen), there is a particular consequence or situation either way.
structure: Verb-た form + ら + (same Verb-た form) + で
structureEn: Verb-た form + ら + (same Verb-た form) + で
explanation: Conveys that if something happens (or doesn’t happen), there is a particular consequence or situation either way. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The pattern VerbたらVerbたで is used for scenarios where doing something creates a certain issue or reaction, and not doing it also creates a different concern. It often shows that no matter which side of the condition you end up on, there’s some related result or trouble.

title: Verb たら きりがない
titleEn: Indicates an endless or unending cycle once an action is started; 'if one starts doing something, it will not stop.'
structure: Verb-た form + ら + きりがない
structureEn: Verb-た form + ら + きりがない
explanation: Indicates an endless or unending cycle once an action is started; 'if one starts doing something, it will not stop.' (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The たらきりがない grammar pattern is used when something negative or undesirable continues indefinitely once it begins. The implication is that you should avoid initiating the action because it’ll just go on and on.

title: Verb たら最後
titleEn: Expresses that once a certain action happens, the situation changes irreversibly—usually in a negative way.
explanation: Expresses that once a certain action happens, the situation changes irreversibly—usually in a negative way. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The たら最後 grammar point indicates that once an action (in た-form) occurs, there is no going back and an unpleasant or unavoidable state follows. Often translated as 'once...then it's all over' or 'the moment...there’s no turning back.'

title: Verb つ Verb つ
titleEn: Expresses two alternating actions happening repeatedly.
structure: Verb-ますstem + つ + Verb-ますstem + つ
structureEn: Verb-ますstem + つ + Verb-ますstem + つ
explanation: Expresses two alternating actions happening repeatedly. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The 〜つ〜つ pattern is used with two verb ます-stems to express two ongoing, repeated or alternating actions. For example, 'push and pull,' 'go and come back,' 'eat and play,' etc. The same subject is typically implied for both verbs.
